original_abstract:
While the self-attention mechanism has revolutionized natural language processing, its transition to computer vision has encountered several structural obstacles. Traditional self-attention often overlooks the intrinsic 2D nature of images by treating them as 1D sequences, incurs prohibitive quadratic computational costs at high resolutions, and focuses exclusively on spatial relationships while neglecting the importance of channel-wise adaptability. 

In this paper, we introduce Large Kernel Attention (LKA), a novel linear attention mechanism designed to overcome these limitations. By strategically decomposing large-kernel convolutions, LKA effectively captures long-range correlations and local contextual information while maintaining high efficiency and enabling both spatial and channel adaptability. Building on this module, we present the Visual Attention Network (VAN), a simple yet highly effective hierarchical backbone. 

Extensive experimental results demonstrate that VAN consistently outperforms state-of-the-art convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) across a wide range of tasks. For instance, VAN-B6 achieves an impressive 87.8% Top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K and establishes a new state-of-the-art performance of 58.2% PQ for panoptic segmentation on the COCO dataset. Furthermore, VAN-B2 significantly surpasses the Swin Transformer in semantic segmentation on ADE20K and object detection on COCO, offering a powerful and lightweight baseline for the computer vision community.
humanized_abstract:
The self-attention mechanism that has revolutionized the field of natural language processing has not seen its equal in computer vision yet, due to several structural challenges. Most self-attention-based models discard the two-dimensional structure of the input images by reducing them to one-dimensional sequences. While such models typically achieve good results on medium-to-low resolution inputs, they suffer from high computational complexity at higher resolutions due to quadratic growth in the number of input elements. Another limitation of existing self-attention based models is that they mainly focus on spatial interactions between pixels and largely ignore the necessity of channel-wise learnability in visual space.

Large Kernel Attention Large Kernel Attention (LKA) is a linear attention mechanism proposed to address the problems mentioned above. With clever decompositions of large-kernel convolutions, LKA learns to capture long-range relationships and local context effectively while maintaining efficiency and spatial and channel adaptivities. Consequently, a Visual Attention Network (VAN) is proposed as a simple yet highly efficient hierarchical backbone based on LKA.

As demonstrated by a large number of experiments, VAN is shown to be stronger than many state-of-the-art models, including most of the widely used CNNs and ViTs. Such as, VAN-B6 attains the state-of-the-art result of 87.8% Top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K, and also sets a new state-of-the-art of 58.2% on PQ of the panoptic segmentation on the COCO dataset. In semantic segmentation on ADE20K and object detection on COCO, VAN-B2 outperforms the state-of-the-art Swin Transformer. Thus, a versatile yet simple baseline is provided for the vision community.

Most self-attention-based models discard the two-dimensional structure of the input images by reducing them to one-dimensional sequences. While such models typically achieve good results on medium-to-low resolution inputs, they suffer from high computational complexity at higher resolutions due to quadratic growth in the number of input elements. Another limitation of existing self-attention based models is that they mainly focus on spatial interactions between pixels and largely ignore the necessity of channel-wise learnability in visual space.

Large Kernel Attention Large Kernel Attention (LKA) is a linear attention mechanism proposed to address the problems mentioned above. With clever decompositions of large-kernel convolutions, LKA learns to capture long-range relationships and local context effectively while maintaining efficiency and spatial and channel adaptivities. Consequently, a Visual Attention Network (VAN) is proposed as a simple yet highly efficient hierarchical backbone based on LKA.

As demonstrated by a large number of experiments, VAN is shown to be stronger than many state-of-the-art models, including most of the widely used CNNs and ViTs. Such as, VAN-B6 attains the state-of-the-art result of 87.8% Top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K, and also sets a new state-of-the-art of 58.2% on PQ of the panoptic segmentation on the COCO dataset. In semantic segmentation on ADE20K and object detection on COCO, VAN-B2 outperforms the state-of-the-art Swin Transformer. Thus, a versatile yet simple baseline is provided for the vision community.
document.input:
While the self-attention mechanism has revolutionized natural language processing, its transition to computer vision has encountered several structural obstacles. Traditional self-attention often overlooks the intrinsic 2D nature of images by treating them as 1D sequences, incurs prohibitive quadratic computational costs at high resolutions, and focuses exclusively on spatial relationships while neglecting the importance of channel-wise adaptability. 

In this paper, we introduce Large Kernel Attention (LKA), a novel linear attention mechanism designed to overcome these limitations. By strategically decomposing large-kernel convolutions, LKA effectively captures long-range correlations and local contextual information while maintaining high efficiency and enabling both spatial and channel adaptability. Building on this module, we present the Visual Attention Network (VAN), a simple yet highly effective hierarchical backbone. 

Extensive experimental results demonstrate that VAN consistently outperforms state-of-the-art convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) across a wide range of tasks. For instance, VAN-B6 achieves an impressive 87.8% Top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K and establishes a new state-of-the-art performance of 58.2% PQ for panoptic segmentation on the COCO dataset. Furthermore, VAN-B2 significantly surpasses the Swin Transformer in semantic segmentation on ADE20K and object detection on COCO, offering a powerful and lightweight baseline for the computer vision community.
